title: State Management
objectives: Know the main approaches and trade-offs
Pick the right tool for a screen

sections.markdown: State management is how you store, update and share data that affects the UI. The key split: ephemeral (local widget) state via setState, and app/shared state via a state-management solution.

There's no single 'best' — the question is fit. setState for local UI; Provider/Riverpod for most shared state; BLoC when you want explicit, testable event→state flows and clear separation. Volume 5 compares them in depth.
Lifting all state to one giant global store, or rebuilding the whole screen on every change. Scope state to where it's used and rebuild granularly (Consumer/Selector/select).
Keep business logic out of widgets. Whatever tool you choose, separate UI from logic (ViewModel/Notifier/BLoC) so it's testable and the UI stays dumb.

flashcards.back: Ephemeral = local to one widget (setState); app state = shared across the app (Provider/Riverpod/BLoC).
Rebuilding only what changed (Consumer/Selector) avoids wasteful whole-screen rebuilds.
Outside widgets — in a Notifier/BLoC/ViewModel — for testability.

quiz.explain: Ephemeral UI state local to one widget.
BLoC maps input events to output states via streams.

options: Rebuilding the whole screen on every change

challenges.text: Build a counter with setState, then with Provider.
Share auth state across screens with Riverpod.
Implement a search feature as a Cubit/BLoC with loading/error/data states.
Refactor a screen to rebuild only changed parts using Selector/select and measure rebuilds.

interviewQuestions: How do you avoid unnecessary rebuilds?
How do you test state logic?
